body.title: /review filing-time: load palace rules + positive checks
body.result: /review SKILL.md Mode A Check 7 loads palace ticket_design_checks, iterates each check, emits failure_message as AMEND finding. Replaces old Test-plan check (subsumed). Commit b11ea1fa. Unblocks T-review-build-tightness-grade.
body.description:
Extend /review filing-time mode (Mode A, called by /decided and /fixit) to load theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks/* from the palace and verify each ticket POSITIVELY against them — does the ticket address the check? Today /review runs generic checks (duplicate, size, scope-creep, test-plan, HIGH-inertia). After this change, /review also asks: 'does this ticket name its test plan?', 'does it name affected files?', 'does it address no-sqlite?', etc.

Scope boundary: extend Mode A only. Do NOT touch standalone plan/code/PR review modes (Mode B) in this ticket.

Verdict mapping: a missed applicable check -> AMEND with the check's failure_message. Multiple misses -> list them in findings.

Why: scaffold-not-correct. Rules become scaffold only when verified at filing. Currently rules live in palace but /review does not read them.

Gated on T-palace-ticket-design-rules.

test_plan: file a ticket missing a test plan, confirm /review AMENDs with the right message; file one addressing all applicable checks, confirm PASS.
